name: Martin Winterkorn
bio: Martin Winterkorn served as the CEO of Volkswagen AG from 2007 until his resignation in September 2015 following the revelation of the emissions scandal.
_evidence: As CEO during the scandal, he was the primary figurehead responsible for the company's culture and oversight, eventually resigning due to the crisis.

name: Matthias Müller
bio: Matthias Müller was the former CEO of Porsche who took over as CEO of Volkswagen AG in the immediate aftermath of the Dieselgate scandal.
_evidence: He was tasked with leading the company's recovery, rebranding efforts, and the strategic pivot toward electric vehicles to restore public trust.

name: Alexander Dobrindt
bio: Alexander Dobrindt served as the German Federal Minister of Transport and Digital Infrastructure during the height of the Dieselgate scandal.
_evidence: He oversaw the government investigation into Volkswagen and faced public pressure to hold the company accountable for emissions violations.
